title: Roche scraps two Ionis-partnered Huntington’s drugs
summary: The Swiss pharmaceutical giant told patient groups tominersen didn't delay disease progression in one trial, while a study of a second drug was scrapped because of a safety signal in animal testing.

title: Whistleblower takes on health insurance and FDA commissioner candidates
summary: This week on "The Readout LOUD" podcast: Why does getting family health insurance through a job cost as much as buying a new car? And who might be the new…

title: GSK ends brain drug alliance with Alector
summary: Clinical setbacks derailed what was once a potentially $2.2 billion deal that marked GSK's return to neuroscience.

title: Outbreak of diarrhea-causing parasite grows to more than 1,000 cases
summary: An outbreak of diarrhea-causing parasite has grown to more than 1,000 cases, making it one of the nation’s largest such outbreaks in years.

title: STAT+: ARPA-H launches $160 million effort to develop custom gene editing drugs
summary: An ARPA-H program to jump-start custom gene editing treatments was delayed by a change in administrations. Now seven labs will get sizable grants.

title: STAT+: FDA finalists under review
summary: Three top contenders to run the FDA are under review by the White House

title: STAT+: Prime wins Beam arbitration, clearing path to clinic
summary: AstraZeneca, Ionis report major trial failure, White House pushes for American drug manufacturing, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about FDA commissioner contenders, a heart drug failure, and more
summary: The White House is reviewing a list of three top contenders for the FDA commissioner post

title: STAT+: OpenEvidence backs study that finds it beats LLMs
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: Dueling research pitting OpenEvidence against general purpose LLMs, use of Dexcom's CGM in children, and more.

title: HHS plan to speed drug research won’t remove ‘self-inflicted’ delays
summary: U.S. clinical trials continue to lose ground to China, but is Operation Trialblazer enough to help America catch up?

title: AstraZeneca, Ionis drug fails big heart disease study in major setback
summary: Eplontersen’s failure in transthyretin-mediated amyloidosis cardiomyopathy caught Wall Street analysts by surprise and boosted the outlook of rival drugs from Alnylam and BridgeBio Pharma.

title: Who’s going to run the FDA?
summary: RFK Jr.'s list of Covid vaccine injuries, a father's quest to cure his daughter, and more health news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: 931 days. The drug approval scandal hiding in plain sight
summary: Two years, six months, and 18 days have elapsed since Northwest Biotherapeutics submitted its brain cancer treatment for regulatory approval.

title: STAT+: AstraZeneca, Ionis report major trial failure with heart disease drug
summary: AstraZeneca and Ionis reported a notable trial failure Thursday in a heart disease that's become an increasingly competitive market for drugmakers.

title: What happens on ‘MAHA Monday’?
summary: STAT's Alex Hogan and Chelsea Cirruzzo stopped by "MAHA Monday" at the Great American State Fair. Watch their report in this week's STATus Report.

title: STAT+: The quest to save Grace — and clear the way for rare disease patients everywhere
summary: How a father’s quest may shape the future of rare disease gene therapy.

title: Opinion: Who benefits from classifying obesity as a disease?
summary: “The new wave of obesity pharmacotherapy has leaned heavily on disease framing,” writes Max Moser, drawing comparisons to the antidepressant revolution.

title: Opinion: Your grandparents are using cannabis. Doctors can help them do it safely
summary: Over the past 25 years, I have treated thousands of medical cannabis patients with extremely few ill effects, one doctor writes.

title: STAT+: RFK Jr. plans to create list of injuries caused by Covid-19 vaccines
summary: It's not clear what conditions may make the list, but outside experts are watching it closely.

title: STAT+: White House reviewing top contenders to lead FDA
summary: The White House is reviewing finalists for FDA commissioner, suggesting the highly anticipated decision may come soon.

title: STAT+: Mass General Brigham, nurses called to talk at State House amid biggest nursing strike in Mass.
summary: The Brigham, a nationally renowned Harvard teaching hospital, and MGB have have unsuccessfully been negotiating a new contract for months.

title: STAT+: In private meeting, Trump officials push to onshore generic drugmaking
summary: Marco Rubio, RFK Jr., and Chris Klomp are urging industry leaders to make more medicines in the U.S.

title: Bryan Johnson’s chronic disease is notoriously difficult to diagnose
summary: Why longevity entrepreneur Bryan Johnson’s chronic disease is notoriously difficult to diagnose.

title: AstraZeneca, GSK intensify China ties with Sino Biopharm deals
summary: The U.K. drugmakers added to a series of recent tie-ups with Chinese counterparts amid broader scrutiny of such transactions in the U.S.

title: Affordable Care Act insurers want more premium increases as enrollment sags
summary: For the second year in a row, many Affordable Care Act insurers are proposing double-digit premium increases, driven by rising medical costs as well as federal policy changes.

title: Prime wins gene editing dispute; Saol claims latest post-Makary FDA reversal
summary: An arbitration panel ruled Prime didn’t breach a deal with Beam by developing a rare disease treatment. Elsewhere, European regulators are using a pancreatic cancer drug to test a new framework for…

title: Oberland pours up to $400M into MeiraGTx eye gene therapies
summary: The funding deal is a vote of confidence in the sales potential of multiple prospects in late-stage development — one of which MeiraGTx recently reacquired from J&J.

title: Kailera says obesity pill succeeds in late-stage trial in China
summary: The drug spurred about 10% weight loss over 10 months, though that finding came alongside high rates of gastrointestinal side effects that “alarmed” one analyst.

title: Vertex to acquire Crinetics in $10B foray into endocrine disease drugs
summary: Vertex claimed the two drugs in the deal could eventually bring in more than $5 billion annually. Still, the high price it paid stirred debate among analysts and investors.

title: Vera Therapeutics wins FDA nod for closely watched kidney disease drug
summary: The clearance marks another step forward in treatment for IgA nephropathy and sets up a commercial battle with Otsuka, which brought a similar medication to market last year.
